goal: mainline status as daily entry point: unsealed drafts + recent sealed + suggestions blocks

summary.title: mainline status as daily entry point: unsealed drafts + recent sealed + suggestions
summary.what: Three additive blocks on StatusResult and the CLI render path. UnsealedDrafts walks all drafts in drafting/sealed_local state across branches and surfaces orphans (excluding the active one already shown via ActiveIntent). RecentSealed picks the top N merged intents from the view by sealed_at desc (default 3, cross-actor included). Suggestions reads the assembled status and produces state-derived next-step CLI commands — drafting suggests append+seal, sealed_local suggests publish, idle+orphan suggests checkout the orphan branch, clean suggests start. Sync-stale and coverage-gaps append regardless. 5 focused engine-layer tests covering each branch of the switch + the dedup invariant + cross-actor recent-sealed.
summary.why: Per the rc7 follow-up message, status was diagnosed as the right next product investment after trace landed (over hooks expansion). trace explains a single intent post-hoc; status should be the daily working entrypoint. The pre-this-PR status was a state dump — branch / actor / sync stale / coverage rollup — but nothing told you "what should I do next" or "you have work on another branch you forgot about". This PR turns status into an answer rather than a query.
summary.user_goal: Make `mainline status` the actual command users (and agents) start their day with — answers who am I, what thread, do I have unsealed work elsewhere, what just landed, what should I do next.

decisions.point: Suggestions as a switch-statement vs a rule engine
decisions.chose: simple switch with append-regardless guards for orthogonal concerns (sync-stale, coverage-gaps)
decisions.rationale: Five lifecycle phases, one suggested action each. A rule engine would over-engineer it for the natural-language quality of the suggestions. The orthogonal axes (sync-stale, coverage) append after the switch rather than nesting inside; clearer to reason about.

decisions.point: UnsealedDrafts excludes vs includes the active draft on current branch
decisions.rationale: Active draft already renders via the existing Intent: line in the main rollup. Including it again under "Unsealed intents (other branches)" would be confusing and wrong-named. The block is specifically for orphans elsewhere.
Three fits inline without scrolling on a typical terminal. Users wanting more run mainline log. Adding a config knob for one preference feels premature; revisit if dogfood reveals real disagreement.

decisions.point: RecentSealed cross-actor or just my own
decisions.rationale: Status answers "what is the team state right now"; my own merges are visible to me through other channels (git log of my own commits). The recent-sealed view earns its keep when it shows what teammates landed since I last looked.

On a clean repo with no orphans + no team activity, status stays as terse as before. Empty "Unsealed intents: (none)" lines would just be noise. The blocks are progressive disclosure: when there is signal, surface it; when there is not, stay quiet.

rejected.alternative: Add a separate `mainline daily` command for the rich view
rejected.reason: Two commands for one mental model — agents and humans default to status; making them learn a second name is friction. status absorbing this is the right scope.

rejected.alternative: Show all proposed intents in the main block
rejected.reason: list-proposals already does that, and a long list would push the actionable Suggestions block out of view. The N=3 RecentSealed cap is the natural compromise.

rejected.alternative: Make Suggestions a structured array with action+reason+command
rejected.reason: Over-engineered for v1. JSON callers can parse the strings; CLI users read them as one-liners. If structured suggestions become load-bearing for tooling later, schema-bump then.

rejected.alternative: Walk all branches via git rev-list to find unsealed drafts
rejected.reason: The drafts directory is the source-of-truth for unsealed intents — no need to consult git history. Listing drafts is O(N) over a typically small directory.

risks: Suggestions are deterministic from state — if the state is misleading (e.g., draft exists but on a force-deleted branch), the suggestion to checkout that branch fails. Acceptable: the user sees the failure clearly and can clean up. Better than no suggestion.
On a very large repo with hundreds of merged intents, view.Intents iteration in collectRecentSealed is O(N). Currently negligible (50 intents max in dogfood); if it grows past 1000 we revisit.

UnsealedDrafts AgeSeconds depends on draft.CreatedAt being set. Legacy drafts pre-rc1 may have empty CreatedAt — in which case AgeSeconds = 0 and the rendering shows "0s old" which looks wrong but is honest about the missing data.
followups: When the agent-hooks PR ecosystem matures, sessionStart hook will already inject status output as system context. The Suggestions block becomes a particularly leveraged surface there — the agent reads them as recommended next steps without thinking.
Consider an `unsealed-only` filter or `--detail` flag for terminals that want richer breakdowns. Premature for v1.
Possible future: weight Suggestions by likelihood (drafting + many turns = "ready to seal"; drafting + few turns = "keep going"). Heuristic; out of scope.

architectural_claims: StatusResult is the canonical daily-entry-point payload: in addition to current branch / actor / sync staleness / coverage, it carries unsealed-drafts (cross-branch orphans), recent-sealed (team activity), and suggestions (state-derived next steps).
Suggestions are derived from the rest of StatusResult — no extra git or storage calls — so the status command stays cheap regardless of repo size.
CLI renders new blocks only when populated; clean-state status remains terse.
behavioral_changes: mainline status output now shows unsealed-drafts (when present), recent-sealed (when view has merged intents), and suggestions (always, derived from state).
JSON callers see additive fields on StatusResult: unsealed_drafts, recent_sealed, suggestions.
